_purpose: Scale correctness fixture (T0.3.x 'prove correctness at scale' arc, 2026-06-04). Read+Search pass 1, scope locked with Shahbaz: gate at 100 facts, 1000 as stretch. The 12-fact toy vault proved the correctness core STRUCTURALLY; this fixture proves it survives when the right answer must win against ~80 unrelated distractor facts. Planted facts carry ground truth; the harness (scale_eval.rs) pads to `scale` with the deterministic t029 distractor generator and SCORES read + search against `expect`.
_scoring: Per query the harness runs BOTH adapter.read() (recall-first read: reranker + the -2.5 no-signal floor) and adapter.search() (recall-first hybrid). It emits a scorecard: read abstention confusion matrix (false-abstain = the cliff / false-answer = over-correction), read recall@k + rank of the target, search recall@k, and a near-miss 'leak' diagnostic (whether the must_exclude fact appears and at what rank vs the target). Characterization only for now (no hard pass/fail gate) — we read the numbers, calibrate the -2.5 floor at scale, THEN pin gates. Per [[anchor-on-measured-not-projected]].
_boundaries_note: Planted facts live in 'personal'; queries authorize all of [personal, work, tools] so the FULL padded pool (planted + distractors) is in retrieval scope — the maximally-adversarial recall setting. The t029 distractor bed is office/facilities-themed; broadening distractor texture to personal-life noise is a follow-up, noted not done.

planted.content: The user's favourite cuisine is Japanese.
The user usually skips breakfast on weekdays.
The user has a golden retriever named Biscuit.
The user volunteers at an animal shelter on Saturdays.
The user works as a structural engineer.
The user is studying for a project management certification.
The user starts every morning with a flat white.
The user gave up energy drinks last year.
The user cycles to the office most mornings.
The user finds the morning traffic on the bridge frustrating.
Plays the cello in a community orchestra on Sunday afternoons.
The user listens to jazz while working.
The user owns a Rivian R1T.
The user takes the train into the city on Fridays.
The user is fluent in Mandarin and English.
The user studied comparative literature at university.
The user runs ten kilometres three times a week.
The user keeps a detailed food diary.
The user settled in Porto after years of moving around.
The user flies to Madrid for work most months.
The user works primarily in a dark-themed editor and finds light themes straining.
The user collects vintage mechanical keyboards.
